E0001.title: Invalid character
E0001.help: Remove the illegal character or use a valid encoding
E0001.error_output:
error[E0001]: Invalid character
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = ¥100;
  |     ^ illegal character '¥'

E0002.title: Invalid number literal
E0002.help: Check the format of the number literal
E0002.error_output:
error[E0002]: Invalid number literal
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = 1_2_3_;
  |     ^ invalid digit

E0003.title: Unterminated string
E0003.message: String literal is missing closing quote
E0003.help: Add the closing quote for the string
E0003.error_output:
error[E0003]: Unterminated string
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = "hello world;
  |     ^ unterminated string

E0004.title: Invalid character literal
E0004.help: Character literals must contain exactly one character
E0004.error_output:
error[E0004]: Invalid character literal
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = 'ab';
  |     ^ character literal contains multiple characters

E0010.title: Expected token
E0010.help: Check the syntax and add the expected token
E0010.error_output:
error[E0010]: Expected token
 --> example.yx:1:7
  |
1 | x = 1, 2;
  |       ^ expected ',' or ';'

E0011.title: Unexpected token
E0011.help: Remove or replace the unexpected token
E0011.error_output:
error[E0011]: Unexpected token
 --> example.yx:1:7
  |
1 | x = 1 @ 2;
  |       ^ unexpected token '@'

E0012.title: Invalid syntax
E0012.message: Expression or statement has syntax error
E0012.help: Check the syntax of the expression or statement
E0012.error_output:
error[E0012]: Invalid syntax
 --> example.yx:1:3
  |
1 | if x { }
  |    ^ missing condition

E0013.title: Mismatched brackets
E0013.message: Parentheses, brackets, or braces are mismatched
E0013.template: Mismatched {bracket_type}: opened at line {open_line}, column {open_col}, not closed
E0013.help: Ensure all brackets are properly closed
E0013.error_output:
error[E0013]: Mismatched brackets
 --> example.yx:1:1
  |
1 | x = (1 + 2;
  |     ^ unclosed parenthesis

E0014.title: Missing semicolon
E0014.help: Add a semicolon at the end of the statement
E0014.error_output:
error[E0014]: Missing semicolon
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = 1
  |       ^ missing semicolon

E1001.title: Unknown variable
E1001.help: Check if the variable name is spelled correctly, or define it first
E1001.error_output:
error[E1001]: Unknown variable
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = y + 1;
  |       ^ undefined variable 'y'

E1002.title: Type mismatch
E1002.message: Expected type does not match actual type
E1002.help: Use the correct type or add a type conversion
E1002.error_output:
error[E1002]: Type mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:12
  |
1 | x: Int = "hello";
  |            ^ expected 'Int', found 'String'

E1003.title: Unknown type
E1003.help: Check if the type name is spelled correctly
E1003.error_output:
error[E1003]: Unknown type
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x: MyType = 1;
  |     ^ undefined type 'MyType'

E1010.title: Parameter count mismatch
E1010.message: Function call arguments do not match definition
E1010.help: Check the number of arguments in the function call
E1010.error_output:
error[E1010]: Parameter count mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:1
  |
1 | add(1, 2, 3);
  |     ^ function expects 2 arguments, found 3

E1011.title: Parameter type mismatch
E1011.help: Ensure the argument types match the function signature
E1011.error_output:
error[E1011]: Parameter type mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | add("hello", 2);
  |     ^ expected 'Int', found 'String'

E1012.title: Return type mismatch
E1012.help: Ensure the return value matches the declared return type
E1012.error_output:
error[E1012]: Return type mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:19
  |
1 | fn foo() -> Int { "hello" }

E1013.title: Function not found
E1013.help: Check if the function name is spelled correctly
E1013.error_output:
error[E1013]: Function not found
 --> example.yx:1:1
  |
1 | unknown_fn();
  | ^ undefined function 'unknown_fn'

E1020.title: Cannot infer type
E1020.help: Add a type annotation or explicit type arguments
E1020.error_output:
error[E1020]: Cannot infer type
 --> example.yx:1:9
  |
1 | let x = nil;
  |         ^ cannot infer type from 'nil'

E1021.title: Type inference conflict
E1021.message: Multiple constraints lead to type contradiction

error[E1021]: Type inference conflict
 --> example.yx:1:11

E1030.title: Pattern non-exhaustive
E1030.message: Match expression does not cover all cases
E1030.help: Add missing patterns to the match expression
E1030.error_output:
error[E1030]: Pattern non-exhaustive
 --> example.yx:1:1
  |

E1031.help: Remove or modify the unreachable pattern
E1031.error_output:
error[E1031]: Unreachable pattern
 --> example.yx:1:18
  |
1 | match x { 1 => "one", 1 => "another" }
  |                  ^ pattern '1' already covered

E1040.title: Operation not supported

error[E1040]: Operation not supported
 --> example.yx:1:9
  |
1 | "hello" + 1;
  |         ^ operation '+' not supported for 'String'

E1041.message: Array/list index is out of range

E1041.error_output:
error[E1041]: Index out of bounds
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |

error[E1050]: Boolean operand required
 --> example.yx:1:9
  |
1 | let x = 1 && 2;

error[E1051]: Boolean operand required for NOT
 --> example.yx:1:9
  |

 --> example.yx:1:12
  |

  |            ^ cannot access field on 'Int'

E1054.title: Condition type mismatch
E1054.help: Use a boolean expression or convert to boolean
E1054.error_output:
error[E1054]: Condition type mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:4
  |
1 | if 42 { }
  |    ^ condition must be boolean, found 'Int'

E1055.title: Constraint in non-generic context
E1055.message: Constraint type used outside generic context

  |

 --> example.yx:1:8
  |

 --> example.yx:1:9
  |

error[E1070]: Unknown label
 --> example.yx:1:7
  |

  |       ^ unknown label 'unknown'

E1080.title: spawn is only allowed inside @block scope
E1080.help: Wrap this `spawn { ... }` inside `@block { ... }`, or mark the enclosing function as `@block` (current: @{mode}).

E1081.title: `?` is only allowed inside functions returning Result
E1081.help: Change the enclosing function's return type to `Result[T, E]` before using `expr?`.

E1082.title: `?` requires a Result expression
E1082.help: Use `?` only on expressions of type `Result[T, E]`.

E1083.title: Result error type mismatch for `?`
E1083.help: Ensure the error type `E` matches between the current function's return type `Result[_, E]` and the `Result[_, E]` value being unwrapped.

E1090.title: ✨ The Ineffable ✨
E1090.message: You have reached the philosophical boundary of YaoXiang
E1090.help: This is the source of YaoXiang, the edge of language
E1090.zen_message:
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                                                               ║
║   One generates Two, Two generates Three,                      ║
║   Three generates all things.                                  ║
║   In the Beginning, there was Taiji, which begat Liangyi.     ║
║                                                               ║
║   Type: Type = Type                                            ║
║   This is the source of YaoXiang, the edge of language.       ║
║   The compiler falls silent here; philosophy stands still.     ║
║                                                               ║
║   Thank you for reaching the philosophical boundary.          ║
║                                                               ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

E2014.title: Use after move
E2014.template: '{name}' has been moved and cannot be used
E2014.help: The value was moved to a new owner. Use a reference or clone if you need to use it again.

E2015.title: Borrow after move
E2015.help: The value was moved. Use a reference before the move, or clone the value.

E2016.title: Assign to immutable
E2016.help: Declare the variable with `mut` to allow assignment.

E2017.title: Multiple mutable borrows
E2017.template: cannot borrow '{name}' as mutable more than once
E2017.help: Ensure only one mutable reference exists at a time.

E2018.title: Mutable/immutable borrow conflict
E2018.template: cannot borrow '{name}' as mutable (already borrowed as immutable)
E2018.help: Ensure no immutable references exist before creating a mutable reference.

E2019.title: Double drop
E2019.help: Ensure the value is only dropped once.

E2020.title: Use after drop
E2020.help: Use the value before it is dropped, or restructure the code.

E2021.title: Drop moved value
E2021.template: cannot drop '{name}': value has been moved
E2021.help: The value was already moved and cannot be dropped.

E2022.title: Immutable mutation
E2022.help: Declare the variable with `mut` to allow mutation.

E2023.title: Immutable field assignment
E2023.template: cannot assign to immutable field '{field}' of '{struct_name}'
E2023.help: Declare the field with `mut` to allow assignment.

E2024.title: Ref non-owner
E2024.template: cannot create reference to '{name}': value is not owned
E2024.help: The value has been moved or dropped. Ensure the value is still owned.

E2025.title: Reassign non-empty
E2025.template: cannot reassign '{name}': variable has not been moved
E2025.help: Only moved variables can be reassigned.

E2026.title: Consumed not returned
E2026.template: parameter '{name}' is consumed but not returned
E2026.help: Either return the consumed value or use a reference.

E2027.title: Unsafe deref
E2027.template: cannot dereference raw pointer outside unsafe block
E2027.help: Wrap the dereference in an `unsafe` block.

E2028.title: Clone moved value
E2028.template: cannot clone '{name}': value has been moved
E2028.help: The value was already moved. Clone before moving if needed.

E3001.title: Unimplemented expression (IR)
E3001.help: This expression type is not yet supported in IR generation.

E3002.title: Unimplemented statement (IR)
E3002.help: This statement type is not yet supported in IR generation.

E3003.title: Invalid operand (IR)
E3003.help: Check the operand type and usage.

E3004.title: Unsupported iterator
E3004.help: Only range and list iterators are currently supported.

E3005.title: IR generation error
E3005.help: This is an internal error. Please report it.

E3010.title: Unimplemented expression (codegen)
E3010.help: This expression type is not yet supported in code generation.

E3011.title: Unimplemented statement (codegen)
E3011.help: This statement type is not yet supported in code generation.

E3012.title: Unimplemented call (codegen)
E3012.help: This call type is not yet supported in code generation.

E3013.title: Symbol not found (codegen)
E3013.help: Ensure the symbol is defined and accessible.

E3014.title: Register overflow
E3014.help: Reduce the number of local variables or simplify expressions.

E3015.title: Invalid assignment target
E3015.help: Only variables and fields can be assigned to.

E3016.title: Type mismatch (codegen)
E3016.help: This is an internal error. Please report it.

E3017.title: Invalid operand (codegen)
E3017.help: Check the operand type and usage.

E3018.title: Translation error
E3018.help: This is an internal error. Please report it.

E4010.title: Constant division by zero
E4010.help: Ensure the divisor is not zero in constant expressions.

E4011.title: Constant overflow
E4011.help: The constant expression result is too large for the type.

E4012.title: Constant recursion too deep
E4012.template: constant evaluation exceeded maximum recursion depth {limit}
E4012.help: Simplify the constant expression to reduce recursion.

E4013.title: Non-constant function
E4013.help: Only functions marked as constant can be used in constant expressions.

E4014.title: Constant evaluation failed
E4014.help: Ensure the expression can be evaluated at compile time.

E4015.title: Loop may not terminate
E4015.template: loop may not terminate: no strictly decreasing measure detected in the loop body
E4015.help: Ensure the loop variable moves monotonically toward the termination condition, e.g. while i < n { i += 1 }.

E4016.title: Recursion may not terminate
E4016.template: recursion may not terminate: parameters of '{func}' do not strictly decrease in the recursive call
E4016.help: Ensure the recursive call uses smaller arguments, e.g. factorial(n-1).

E4017.title: Measure not strictly decreasing
E4017.template: measure '{measure}' does not strictly decrease on all loop paths
E4017.help: Ensure the variable moves toward the termination condition in all branches of the loop body.

E5007.title: Module exports
E5007.help: Available exports are listed in the error message

E6006.title: Function not found (runtime)
E6006.help: Ensure the function is defined and spelled correctly

E6007.title: Runtime error
E6007.help: Check the error message for details

E8004.title: Unimplemented feature
E8004.help: This feature is not yet implemented.

E0015.title: Expected identifier
E0015.help: Provide a valid identifier at this position
E0015.error_output:
error[E0015]: Expected identifier
 --> example.yx:1:4
  |
1 | fn () {}
  |    ^ expected function name

E0016.title: Expected expression
E0016.help: Provide a valid expression at this position
E0016.error_output:
error[E0016]: Expected expression
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = ;
  |     ^ expected expression after '='

E0017.title: Old syntax rejected
E0017.message: Old syntax is no longer supported
E0017.template: Old syntax '{old}' is no longer supported. Use '{new}' instead
E0017.help: Use the new syntax as shown in the suggestion
E0017.error_output:
error[E0017]: Old syntax rejected
 --> example.yx:1:1
  |
1 | Type[T]
  | ^^^^ old syntax; use 'Type' alone

E0018.title: Keyword as name
E0018.message: Keyword cannot be used as a variable or expression name
E0018.template: '{keyword}' is a keyword and cannot be used as a name
E0018.help: Use a different identifier instead of the keyword
E0018.error_output:
error[E0018]: Keyword as name
 --> example.yx:1:1
  |
1 | if = 1;
  | ^^ 'if' is a keyword

E0019.title: Parameter count mismatch
E0019.message: Function parameter count does not match
E0019.help: Ensure the number of parameters matches the signature
E0019.example: f: (a: Int) -> Int = (a, b) => a + b
E0019.error_output:
error[E0019]: Parameter count mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:20
  |
1 | f: (a: Int) -> Int = (a, b) => a + b
  |                    ^ expected 1 parameter, found 2

E0020.title: Parameter name mismatch
E0020.message: Parameter name does not match the signature
E0020.template: Parameter name mismatch at position {position}: expected '{expected}', found '{found}'
E0020.help: Use matching parameter names or omit the lambda head
E0020.error_output:
error[E0020]: Parameter name mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:20
  |
1 | f: (a: Int) -> Int = (b) => b
  |                    ^ expected 'a', found 'b'

E0021.title: Expected token after
E0021.message: Parser expected a specific token after a context
E0021.help: Add the expected token in the correct position
E0021.error_output:
error[E0021]: Expected token after
 --> example.yx:1:7
  |
1 | mut x Int
  |       ^ expected ':' after 'mut'

E0022.title: Enum brace syntax required
E0022.message: Enum variants must use brace syntax
E0022.template: Enum variants must use brace syntax: {| variant1 | variant2 | ... }
E0022.help: Wrap enum variants in braces with pipe separators
E0022.error_output:
error[E0022]: Enum brace syntax required
 --> example.yx:1:9
  |
1 | Color = red | green | blue
  |         ^ use brace syntax: { red | green | blue }
